summary: Large gm_1601 decomp/matching PR split out from jj/the-big-gm-pr. It replaces many gm_1601.c stubs with C implementations, tightens gm_1601.h prototypes, and exposes a few previously padded struct fields in gm/types.h. The work covers match-end standings/stat accumulation, persistent nametag stats, VS match counters, winner/loser ranking, player/team result merging, special spawn/player setup paths, character/costume selection helpers, and language-dependent name text helpers. The decomp.dev bot reported +1624 matched bytes, 2 new exact matches, and 18 improved unmatched items, with no human review comments in this slice.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: 1205 added lines implementing or improving gm_1601 functions and adding no-inline wrapper tactics.
Prototype cleanup: several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations replaced with concrete return and argument types.
Struct layout refinement: split padding in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and MatchPlayerData into named offset fields used by new code.

what_changed: Added includes needed by the new implementations: placeholder.h, if/ifnametag.h, and pl/plbonus.h.

Implemented or substantially improved many gm_1601.c stubs, including fn_80160DE8, fn_80161C90, fn_80162170, gm_8016260C, fn_80164B48, fn_80165548, fn_801656A8, fn_8016588C, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, gm_80166CCC, fn_80167638, gm_80168710, fn_80169000, fn_80169A84, fn_80169C54, fn_8016A4C8, and gm_8016AC44.
Reworked gm_80160C90's fighter-name selection from an inlined language/alt-name branch into calls through gm_80160980_noinline3 or fn_801609E0_noinline3.
Added fn_80160DE8 text/JObj helper logic for language-specific character names, size lookup from lbl_803B75F8, and HSD_SisLib_803A70A0/HSD_SisLib_803A7548 calls.
Added local struct gm_stats and used it in fn_80161C90 to accumulate persistent player statistics with saturating MIN limits.
Added persistent nametag stat updates in fn_80162170 using GetPersistentNameData, vs_kos, play_time_by_fighter, and sd_count; nametag sentinel 0x78 is skipped.

Implemented match-total counter updates in gm_8016260C using gmMainLib_GetMatchResetCounter, gmMainLib_GetTimeMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetStockMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetCoinMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetBonusMatchTotal, and gmMainLib_8015ED98/EDB0/EDBC.
Implemented player and team winner/loser ranking functions fn_80165AC0 and fn_80165FA4, setting loser nibbles, winners arrays, and winner counts in MatchEnd.
Implemented gm_80166CCC to merge two MatchEnd result structures, including player/team big-loser and small-loser propagation.
Implemented character/costume selection and setup paths around gm_8016A22C, fn_80169A84, fn_80169C54, fn_8016A4C8, and gm_8016AC44.
Replaced one raw gmMainLib_8015EDBC pointer-offset access in gm_80162574 with typed x18 array access.
Updated gm/types.h by exposing lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t fields x8, x9, x1C and MatchPlayerData fields x38 and x3C.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scoped to gm_1601 and related header/type updates, explicitly splitting it out from a larger jj/the-big-gm-pr.
Used layered wrapper functions named _inner, _noinline, _noinline2, and _noinline3 to influence inlining at specific call sites instead of relying only on broad file/function pragmas.
Scoped an existing no-inline pragma with #pragma push/#pragma pop around fn_801697FC, reducing the chance of unintended codegen changes outside that helper.
Converted raw offset math to typed struct access where layout confidence was high, such as gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x18[gm_80164024(arg0)].
Preserved exact-width stat saturation behavior with MIN(..., 0xFFFF) and MIN(..., 0xFFFFFFFFU), which is important for save-data-style counters.
Updated gm_1601.h prototypes in the same PR as the implementations, reducing follow-on type ambiguity for later gm_1601 work.
Exposed only a small number of previously padded fields in shared structs, keeping unknown layout names conservative while enabling direct field use.
decomp_lessons: When semantics are not known, prefer offset-style field names such as x38, x3C, x1C, or unk20 rather than inventing descriptive names.
gm_1601 match-end logic uses both 4-player, 5-team, and 6-entry loops; preserve the original loop bounds instead of normalizing them prematurely.
slot_type != 3 is repeatedly used as the active-player check, while nametag value 0x78 is treated as a persistent-name-data sentinel to skip.
Persistent stat accumulation should be saturated rather than allowed to overflow; this PR uses explicit MIN clamps for u16 and u32 counters.

Header prototype refinement can be useful even when a function is still not fully matched; this slice shows fn_80161154 changed to s32 fn_80161154(MatchEnd*) while the visible C diff still shows only the placeholder marker and call sites.
Struct padding should be split only when code needs the fields and the offsets are clear from use; this PR carved out small fields in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and MatchPlayerData.
Language-dependent UI text helpers may require indirect wrapper calls to avoid different inlining and branch structure in callers.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions keep address-derived gm_8016xxxx or fn_8016xxxx names.
No-inline matching shims use suffixes such as _inner, _noinline, _noinline2, and _noinline3.
Unknown struct members stay offset-based, for example x8, x9, x1C, x38, x3C, unk0, unk4, and unk40.
Local temporary structs can be named by nearby labels when semantics are unknown, as with lbl_803B7A44_t.
Concrete prototypes use project scalar typedefs such as u8, s8, u16, u32, s32, f32, bool, and known structs like MatchEnd and lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.
Semantic enum/type names are reused when already known, such as CKIND_KIRBY, Gm_PKind_Cpu, Gm_PKind_NA, MatchPlayerData, and MatchTeamData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced #pragma dont_inline around gm_801603B0 calls with wrappers gm_801601C4_noinline and gm_80160244_noinline.
Used triple wrapper chains for gm_80160980 and fn_801609E0 to make gm_80160C90 and fn_80160DE8 call through the desired path.
Added gm_80164840_noinline and gm_80169238_noinline wrappers to prevent helper calls from inlining into character/costume selection code.
Added fn_801695BC_noinline, fn_801697FC_noinline, and fn_80169900_noinline wrappers before gm_8016A22C to tune call structure.
Kept explicit casts such as (u8), (s8), (u32), and (s32) in arithmetic and comparisons to match original signedness and truncation.
Used fixed-size stack buffers such as u8 buf[4], u8 handicaps[4], u8 positions[4], and s32 buf[7] in matching-sensitive helper implementations.
Preserved unusual-looking control-flow/codegen patterns in fn_8016588C, including repeated clamp blocks and an unreachable clamp sequence after a break in the default case.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev reported 2 new exact matches: gm_80166CCC (+1140 bytes) and fn_801656A8 (+484 bytes).

decomp.dev reported 18 improvements in unmatched gm_1601 items, including fn_8016A4C8, fn_80161C90, fn_80164B48, fn_80169C54, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, fn_80169000, fn_80162170, fn_8016588C, gm_8016260C, gm_80168710, fn_80167638, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169A84, fn_80165548, fn_80160DE8, gm_80160C90, and .sdata2.
decomp.dev reported one unmatched-item regression: gm_8016A22C lost 4 bytes, going from 75.44% to 74.75%.
follow_up_queries: Check final merged gm_1601.c around fn_80161154: the PR body lists it among matched stubs, but this diff slice only shows a prototype refinement and call sites.
Investigate whether the gm_8016A22C regression reported by decomp.dev was caused by wrapper or pragma changes and whether later PRs recovered it.
Revisit the local gm_stats field names once the persistent nametag/stat-save layout is better understood.
Confirm fn_80162170's s32 declaration against its no-explicit-return body if touching that function again.
Look for opportunities to remove or simplify _noinline wrapper chains once adjacent functions are fully matched.
Consider moving lbl_803B7A44_t or gm_stats into shared headers only after their semantics and reuse are clear.
